entries.statement: Catalog research verified that agent-version:a5c@current composes the expected full stack: AgentCoreImpl, AgentRuntimeImpl, AgentPlatformImpl, and AgentUIImpl.
entries.note: Captures that enrichment did not need replacement layer nodes because no implementation layer gap remained.

entries.statement: Catalog research observed @a5c-ai/babysitter as the public npm metapackage for a5c installation, with latest version 5.0.0 on 2026-05-04.
entries.note: Adds web-researched package provenance for the a5c AgentProduct.

entries.statement: Catalog research observed @a5c-ai/babysitter-sdk as the npm package for Babysitter SDK storage and run-registry primitives, with latest version 5.0.0 on 2026-05-04.
entries.note: Adds web-researched runtime package provenance for a5c.

entries.statement: Catalog research verified the a5c UI model as TUI and CLI surfaces exposing install, configure, plugin browse, model picker, and command palette interaction primitives.
entries.note: Adds research provenance for the currently modeled a5c UI interaction primitives.

entries.value: anthropic-messages openai-responses openai-chat-completions gemini-generate-content openai-compat openrouter github-copilot
entries.statement: Catalog research verified the local a5c core model as a unified transport profile spanning Anthropic Messages, OpenAI Responses, OpenAI Chat Completions, Gemini Generate Content, OpenAI-compatible APIs, OpenRouter, and GitHub Copilot.
entries.note: Adds research provenance for the a5c core transport profile.

entries.value: agent-mux CLI exposes amux leaf subcommands as cli-subcommand InteractionPrimitive records with exit-code metadata.
entries.statement: The agent-mux CLI subcommand surface is grounded in command handler source files and the shared exit-code mapping.

entries.value: @a5c-ai/agent-mux-cli is modeled as a CLI and bin-entrypoint package surface.
entries.statement: The agent-mux CLI package surface is grounded in its package metadata and amux binary entrypoint.

entries.value: agent-mux gateway exposes health, token, run, session, workspace, dispatch, WebSocket, and Kanban HTTP endpoints as APIEndpoint records.
entries.statement: The agent-mux gateway endpoint records are grounded in server route source files and bearer-auth middleware.

entries.value: @a5c-ai/agent-mux-gateway is modeled as the package surface exposing gateway HTTP, WebSocket, and Kanban APIs.
entries.statement: The agent-mux gateway package surface is grounded in package metadata and gateway source files.

entries.value: Harness-mock exposes interactive approval, error, and runtime-hook mock scenarios as test-fixture InteractionPrimitive records.
entries.statement: Harness-mock scenario primitives are grounded in scenario fixture source files and wired from the package surface with exposes_subcommand edges.

entries.value: @a5c-ai/agent-mux-harness-mock is modeled as a test-fixture, bin-entrypoint, and harness-emulator package surface.
entries.statement: The harness-mock package surface is grounded in its package.json package metadata and mock-harness binary entrypoint.

entries.value: agent-mux TUI exposes command palette, prompt input, and model picker as tui-command InteractionPrimitive records.
entries.statement: The agent-mux TUI exposes interactive command primitives backed by command-palette, prompt-input, and model-picker source files.

entries.value: @a5c-ai/agent-mux-tui is modeled as a TUI and bin-entrypoint package surface.
entries.statement: The agent-mux TUI package surface is grounded in its package metadata and amux-tui binary entrypoint.

entries.value: Babysitter platform defines task-schema fields for defineTask id/name/kind/title/description/labels/source/inputs/outputs/io/metadata/execution/node/breakpoint/sleep/subprocess.
entries.statement: Babysitter task schema support is represented by babysitter.platform defines_frontmatter_field edges to task-schema FrontmatterField records derived from defineTask and task types.

entries.value: @a5c-ai/breakpoints-mux is modeled as a breakpoint-runtime package surface.
entries.statement: The breakpoints-mux package surface is grounded in package metadata and runtime source files.

entries.value: breakpoints-mux server backend client methods are modeled as authenticated outbound-client APIEndpoint records for question create/get/cancel/list/answer/claim and expert listing.
entries.statement: The breakpoints-mux server-client endpoint records are grounded in ServerBreakpointBackend source methods.
Claude Code 1.x is launched via the `claude` CLI command.
Claude Opus 4.7 supports a 1,000,000-token context window.
Claude Opus 4.7 supports extended thinking (interleaved chain-of-thought).

Codex runtime controls combine approval policy and sandbox mode: the approval policy determines when Codex must ask before crossing boundaries, and the sandbox defines file and network boundaries for local commands.
entries.note: Supports approvalGatingPrimitive and subprocessSandboxStrategy attributes on the Codex runtime model.

entries.statement: Codex CLI 0.120.0 exposes four `--ask-for-approval` values: `untrusted`, `on-failure`, `on-request`, and `never`. The CLI help marks `on-failure` as deprecated and recommends `on-request` for interactive runs or `never` for non-interactive runs.
entries.note: Supports the current Codex PermissionMode nodes and replaces stale suggest/auto-edit/full-auto approval-mode modeling.

entries.statement: Codex CLI exposes `--ask-for-approval <APPROVAL_POLICY>` values for untrusted, on-failure, on-request, and never modes, plus `--full-auto` and `--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox` convenience launch flags.
entries.note: Adds current command-line help provenance for Codex approval controls and launch recipes.

entries.statement: Codex CLI 0.120.0 documents `--full-auto` as a convenience alias for low-friction sandboxed automatic execution equivalent to `-a on-request --sandbox workspace-write`, and documents `--dangerously-bypass-approvals-and-sandbox` as an extremely dangerous flag that skips confirmations and sandboxing for externally sandboxed environments.
entries.note: Supports Codex LaunchConfig nodes for CLI convenience and bypass flags.

entries.statement: Codex CLI is modeled as OpenAI's local terminal coding agent that can read, change, and run code in the selected workspace, and OpenAI describes it as open source and built in Rust.
entries.note: Adds current OpenAI developer documentation provenance for the Codex AgentProduct description.

entries.statement: Codex CLI slash commands provide keyboard-first control in interactive sessions, including model, fast, personality, permissions, agent, status, MCP, diff, and theme commands.
entries.note: Adds current OpenAI developer documentation provenance for Codex CLI interaction primitives.

entries.statement: Codex reads user-level configuration from ~/.codex/config.toml and supports project-scoped overrides with .codex/config.toml files.
entries.note: Adds current OpenAI developer documentation provenance for Codex platform configuration files.

entries.value: codex and codex-reply JSON schemas from Codex MCP tools/list
entries.statement: The Codex MCP server in @openai/codex 0.120.0 exposes codex and codex-reply tools. The codex tool requires prompt and returns threadId plus content; codex-reply requires prompt, accepts threadId and the deprecated conversationId alias, and returns threadId plus content.
entries.note: Supports exact inputSchema and outputSchema attributes on the Codex MCP ToolDescriptor nodes.

entries.value: stdio and streamable-http capable MCP integration
entries.statement: Codex supports Model Context Protocol integrations in the CLI and IDE extension, including STDIO server configuration and OpenAI-documented multi-agent MCP server usage.
entries.note: Adds current OpenAI developer documentation provenance for Codex MCP runtime support.

entries.statement: Codex CLI 0.120.0 exposes three `--sandbox` values: `read-only`, `workspace-write`, and `danger-full-access`. These modes are modeled as Codex-specific Sandbox nodes and launch recipes.
entries.note: Supports Codex Sandbox nodes and --sandbox LaunchConfig recipes.

entries.value: Cohere chat-stream emits message/content/tool-call start, delta, end, and message-end stream events modeled as ProtocolMessage nodes.
entries.statement: Cohere chat-stream event taxonomy is modeled through provider:cohere emits_message_type edges to the Cohere ProtocolMessage records.

entries.value: Cohere v2 provider exposes chat, chat-stream, embed, rerank, and models API surfaces.
entries.statement: Cohere v2 provider endpoint modeling is grounded in the Cohere chat/chat-stream API documentation and the existing provider endpoint map.

entries.value: Cursor platform defines description, globs, and alwaysApply frontmatter fields for .mdc rules.
entries.statement: Cursor rule frontmatter support is represented by cursor.platform defines_frontmatter_field edges to the cursor-rule FrontmatterField records.

entries.value: @a5c-ai/hooks-mux-core is modeled as the package surface exposing normalized hook payload schemas.
entries.statement: The hooks-mux core package surface is grounded in package metadata and core package source files.

entries.value: hooks-mux core exposes shared context specs for unified hook events, execution context, hook results, merge results, phase mapping, session state, harness detection, and propagation options.
entries.statement: The hooks-mux shared payload schema records are grounded in the public core type, merge, discovery, and propagation source files.

entries.value:
Bearer tokens via `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on both the SSE
GET and the POST endpoint. The 2025-06-18 OAuth Resource Server
formalisation does not apply — this transport was deprecated before
that revision was published.
entries.statement:
mcp-transport:http-sse authentication is Bearer tokens via `Authorization: Bearer <token>` on both the SSE
GET and the POST endpoint. The 2025-06-18 OAuth Resource Server
formalisation does not apply — this transport was deprecated before
that revision was published.
.
entries.note: Evidence binding for MCPTransport attributes.

entries.value:
Same `initialize` request/response payloads as stdio and Streamable
HTTP — only the delivery path differs (POST request, SSE-event response).
entries.statement:
mcp-transport:http-sse capabilityNegotiation is Same `initialize` request/response payloads as stdio and Streamable
HTTP — only the delivery path differs (POST request, SSE-event response).
.
entries.note: Evidence binding for MCPTransport attributes.

entries.value:
Two-endpoint design (legacy):
  1. Client opens GET on the SSE endpoint; server responds with an
     `endpoint` SSE event whose `data:` is the URL the client must
     POST JSON-RPC requests to (typically a per-session URL).
  2. Client POSTs `initialize` to the announced endpoint; response
     is delivered asynchronously as an SSE `message` event on the
     open GET stream.
  3. Client POSTs `notifications/initialized`. Session is ready.
  4. Shutdown is by closing the SSE GET; no explicit DELETE.
entries.statement:
mcp-transport:http-sse connectionLifecycle is Two-endpoint design (legacy):
  1. Client opens GET on the SSE endpoint; server responds with an
     `endpoint` SSE event whose `data:` is the URL the client must
     POST JSON-RPC requests to (typically a per-session URL).
  2. Client POSTs `initialize` to the announced endpoint; response
     is delivered asynchronously as an SSE `message` event on the
     open GET stream.
  3. Client POSTs `notifications/initialized`. Session is ready.
  4. Shutdown is by closing the SSE GET; no explicit DELETE.
.
entries.note: Evidence binding for MCPTransport attributes.
Evidence binding for MCPTransport attributes.

entries.value:
Server-to-client notifications/requests/responses all flow on the
single SSE GET stream as `message` events. Client-to-server notifications
are POSTed to the announced endpoint and acknowledged with 202 Accepted.
entries.statement:
mcp-transport:http-sse notificationModel is Server-to-client notifications/requests/responses all flow on the
single SSE GET stream as `message` events. Client-to-server notifications
are POSTed to the announced endpoint and acknowledged with 202 Accepted.
.
entries.note: Evidence binding for MCPTransport attributes.

entries.value:
No formal session-resumption mechanism in the spec. On stream loss
the client is expected to reopen the SSE GET and run `initialize`
again; the server will issue a fresh per-session POST endpoint.
This lack of resumption is one of the reasons the 2025-03-26
revision deprecated this transport in favour of Streamable HTTP.
entries.statement:
mcp-transport:http-sse reconnectPolicy is No formal session-resumption mechanism in the spec. On stream loss
the client is expected to reopen the SSE GET and run `initialize`
again; the server will issue a fresh per-session POST endpoint.
This lack of resumption is one of the reasons the 2025-03-26
revision deprecated this transport in favour of Streamable HTTP.
.
entries.note: Evidence binding for MCPTransport attributes.
Evidence binding for MCPTransport attributes.
Evidence binding for MCPTransport attributes.
Evidence binding for MCPTransport attributes.
Evidence binding for MCPTransport attributes.
Evidence binding for MCPTransport attributes.

entries.value:
SSE event stream on the GET, each event `event: message\ndata: <json>\n\n`
(or `event: endpoint` for the initial endpoint announcement).
Per-message JSON-RPC framing on the POST endpoint (one message per
request body). Standard JSON-RPC message-id discipline.
entries.statement:
mcp-transport:http-sse streamingFraming is SSE event stream on the GET, each event `event: message\ndata: <json>\n\n`
(or `event: endpoint` for the initial endpoint announcement).
Per-message JSON-RPC framing on the POST endpoint (one message per
request body). Standard JSON-RPC message-id discipline.
.
entries.note: Evidence binding for MCPTransport attributes.

entries.value: Mistral chat messages are modeled as system, user, assistant, tool, stream chunk, and stream terminal ProtocolMessage records.

entries.value: Ollama native API responses are modeled as chat, chat-stream, generate, generate-stream, and embed ProtocolMessage records.
entries.statement: Ollama native protocol modeling is represented by provider:ollama emits_message_type edges to Ollama ProtocolMessage records.

entries.value: Ollama native HTTP API exposes chat, generate, embed, tags, and show endpoints on the local server.
entries.statement: Ollama provider endpoint modeling is grounded in the Ollama HTTP API documentation and the existing provider endpoint map.

entries.value: OpenAI Agents SDK handoffs are modeled as tool-call based delegation to another Agent.
entries.statement: OpenAI Agents SDK core handoff modeling uses tool-call delegation because handoffs are represented as tools to the model.

entries.value: OpenAI Agents SDK Runner streaming emits raw response, run item, and agent updated stream events modeled as JournalEvent nodes.
entries.statement: OpenAI Agents SDK runtime journal-event modeling is grounded in the Runner streaming event taxonomy.
Google DeepMind describes Gemini 3.1 Deep Think as a specialized reasoning mode built on Gemini 3.1 Pro.
Google publishes Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite documentation for a low-cost Gemini 3 series model.
Google DeepMind publishes a Gemini 3.1 Pro model card.
Google DeepMind lists Gemini 3 Flash as a Gemini 3 model for agentic workflows.
Google lists Gemini 3 Pro as a Gemini 3 model.
OpenAI lists GPT-5.4 as an API model with published token pricing.
OpenAI lists GPT-5.4 mini as an API model with published token pricing.
OpenAI lists GPT-5.5 as an API model with published token pricing.
